dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7981 IH: Stop Secret Counseling of Students Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Luna introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Education and Workforce

form.official-title: To amend the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to prohibit funds under such Act from being provided to public elementary and secondary schools that provide counseling, therapy, or guidance related to gender identity to students under 18 years of age, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Stop Secret Counseling of Students Act.

section.header: Prohibition on gender-related counseling in public elementary and secondary schools

subsection.text: Subpart 2 of part F of title VIII of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (20 U.S.C. 7901 et seq.) is amended by adding at the end the following new section:
No funds under this Act may be provided to a public elementary or secondary school that violates the requirements described in subsection (b).
The requirements described in this subsection for a public elementary or secondary school are the following:

paragraph.text: An employee or a contractor may not provide a student under 18 years of age with counseling, therapy, or guidance related to gender identity, including assisting with creating a gender support or social transition plan.
An employee or a contractor may not—

subparagraph.text: encourage students to hide information regarding the gender identity or social transition of the student from parents; or
provide guidance to students on how to hide such information from parents.

subsection.text: A parent of a student may bring a civil action for injunctive relief in any Federal district court of appropriate jurisdiction against a public elementary or secondary school for a violation of a requirement described in subsection (b) by an employee or a contractor of such school with respect to such student.
The table of contents in section 2 of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 is amended by inserting after the item relating to section 8549C the following:
